findings.message: PriorityLevelConfiguration "catch-all" (apiVersion flowcontrol.apiserver.k8s.io/v1beta3) is an apiserver/platform-managed default that still exists at a version removed in Kubernetes 1.32 — usually no direct user action, since kube-apiserver or the cloud provider's control plane recreates its own flowcontrol defaults at the version it currently serves
evidence: reconciled automatically: matched via the apf.kubernetes.io/autoupdate-spec annotation (kube-apiserver bootstrap default) or a platform field manager such as EKS's own eks-internal (cloud-provider-injected default)
catalog source: Kubernetes Deprecated API Migration Guide
findings.remediation: No action needed for this specific object: kube-apiserver or the cloud provider's control plane owns and recreates its own flowcontrol bootstrap defaults at whatever apiVersion it currently serves. If this cluster has custom FlowSchema/PriorityLevelConfiguration objects beyond the defaults, verify those separately — only recognized bootstrap sets (kube-apiserver's own, marked with the apf.kubernetes.io/autoupdate-spec annotation, and known cloud-provider ones) are covered by this note.
findings.priorityReason: Resource or behavior may fail after the target Kubernetes upgrade.

findings.message: ValidatingWebhookConfiguration "vpc-resource-validating-webhook": webhook "vnode.vpc.k8s.aws" (index 1 in .webhooks) matches nodes — a fail-closed webhook here can block node status updates, namespace lifecycle, or PersistentVolume operations that upgrade/maintenance workflows depend on
findings.remediation: Confirm this webhook genuinely needs to validate/mutate this resource. If not, narrow its rules to exclude it.
findings.priorityReason: Upgrade should not begin while workloads, nodes, or critical add-ons are unhealthy.

findings.message: Deployment kube-system/coredns has a nodeSelector/required nodeAffinity satisfied by only 1 node(s) in this cluster today ([redacted node hostname]) — if that node is drained, no other currently-known node can host a replacement pod
findings.remediation: Label additional nodes to match this workload's nodeSelector/nodeAffinity (and taint them consistently if tolerations are also required), or relax the constraint if it's broader than actually needed.
findings.priorityReason: Node drain may fail during maintenance or a managed node group upgrade.

findings.message: EKS add-on "coredns" version v1.11.4-eksbuild.39 has no compatibility catalog entry for target Kubernetes 1.32 — confirm compatibility before starting the upgrade
evidence: catalog source: no catalog entry for provider=eks add-on target
required upgrade order: 3. CoreDNS after VPC CNI and kube-proxy, before storage CSI add-ons
findings.remediation: Verify this EKS managed add-on against provider compatibility metadata before upgrading. Missing catalog coverage is a warning, not proof of incompatibility.

safeFix.label: Safe fix
steps: Verify the add-on version against provider compatibility metadata before upgrading.

findings.priorityReason: Node drain may fail during maintenance or a managed node group upgrade.

findings.message: EKS add-on "kube-proxy" version v1.31.14-eksbuild.18 has no compatibility catalog entry for target Kubernetes 1.32 — confirm compatibility before starting the upgrade
evidence: catalog source: no catalog entry for provider=eks add-on target
required upgrade order: 2. kube-proxy after VPC CNI and before CoreDNS/storage add-ons
findings.remediation: Verify this EKS managed add-on against provider compatibility metadata before upgrading. Missing catalog coverage is a warning, not proof of incompatibility.

steps: Verify the add-on version against provider compatibility metadata before upgrading.

findings.priorityReason: Node drain may fail during maintenance or a managed node group upgrade.

findings.message: Managed node group "ng-small" desired size equals or is below minimum size. Rolling update may have limited disruption headroom.
findings.remediation: Review node group capacity and disruption budgets before upgrade. Consider temporarily increasing desired capacity or otherwise creating eviction headroom for the change window.

safeFix.label: Inspect scaling config
steps: Inspect managed node group scaling and update settings before choosing any capacity change.

findings.priorityReason: Node drain may fail during maintenance or a managed node group upgrade.

findings.message: Managed node group "ng-small" uses a launch template/custom AMI. Validate AMI, bootstrap, kubelet, and launch template upgrade path manually.
findings.remediation: Manually validate the launch template or custom AMI upgrade path, including bootstrap configuration, kubelet version, user data, and AMI release process.

safeFix.label: Inspect node group configuration
steps: Review the launch template/custom AMI before updating the managed node group version.

findings.priorityReason: Upgrade should not begin while workloads, nodes, or critical add-ons are unhealthy.

findings.message: Managed node group "ng-small" reports Kubernetes version 1.31 while target is 1.32. Node kubelet skew is evaluated separately by NODE-001.
evidence: NODE-001 evaluates actual Kubernetes node/kubelet skew separately.
findings.remediation: Use this as provider inventory context. Confirm actual node kubelet skew in NODE-001 findings and update managed node groups in the provider-recommended sequence.
findings.priorityReason: Upgrade should not begin while workloads, nodes, or critical add-ons are unhealthy.

findings.message: EKS upgrade insight "EKS add-on version compatibility" reports UNKNOWN for Kubernetes 1.32. Treat this as AWS-native context and verify with a fresh scan before upgrade.
evidence: reason: Unable to determine version compatibility of EKS add-ons.
recommendation: Upgrade your EKS add-on to a newer version compatible with the next Kubernetes version.

eksUpgradeInsights.name: Deprecated APIs removed in Kubernetes v1.32
eksUpgradeInsights.description: Checks for usage of deprecated APIs that are scheduled for removal in Kubernetes v1.32. Upgrading your cluster before migrating to the updated APIs supported by v1.32 could cause application impact.
eksUpgradeInsights.recommendation: Update manifests and API clients to use newer Kubernetes APIs if applicable before upgrading to Kubernetes v1.32.

eksUpgradeInsights.name: kube-proxy version skew
eksUpgradeInsights.description: Checks version of kube-proxy in cluster to see if upgrade would cause non compliance with supported Kubernetes kube-proxy version skew policy.
eksUpgradeInsights.recommendation: Upgrade your kube-proxy version to match the Kubernetes version of your cluster control plane.

eksUpgradeInsights.name: Amazon Linux 2 compatibility
eksUpgradeInsights.description: Checks if any nodes in the cluster are running Amazon Linux 2. Support for Amazon Linux 2 on Amazon EKS will end on November 26, 2025. Additionally, Amazon EKS will not release Amazon Linux 2 AMIs for Kubernetes version 1.33 and higher
eksUpgradeInsights.recommendation: Migrate all EKS nodes using Amazon Linux 2 AMIs to Bottlerocket or Amazon Linux 2023 AMIs before November 26, 2025, or prior to upgrading to EKS version 1.33.

eksUpgradeInsights.name: Kubelet version skew
eksUpgradeInsights.description: Checks for kubelet versions of worker nodes in the cluster to see if upgrade would cause non compliance with supported Kubernetes kubelet version skew policy.
eksUpgradeInsights.recommendation: Upgrade your worker nodes to match the Kubernetes version of your cluster control plane.

eksUpgradeInsights.name: EKS add-on version compatibility
eksUpgradeInsights.description: Checks version of installed EKS add-ons to ensure they are compatible with the next version of Kubernetes.
eksUpgradeInsights.recommendation: Upgrade your EKS add-on to a newer version compatible with the next Kubernetes version.
addonCompatibilityDetails: kube-proxy compatible versions: v1.31.14-eksbuild.18, v1.31.14-eksbuild.20, v1.32.0-eksbuild.2, v1.32.3-eksbuild.2, v1.32.3-eksbuild.7, v1.32.5-eksbuild.2, v1.32.6-eksbuild.2, v1.32.6-eksbuild.6, v1.32.6-eksbuild.8, v1.32.6-eksbuild.12, v1.32.9-eksbuild.2, v1.32.11-eksbuild.2, v1.32.11-eksbuild.5, v1.32.13-eksbuild.2, v1.32.13-eksbuild.5, v1.32.13-eksbuild.8, v1.32.13-eksbuild.11, v1.32.13-eksbuild.14, v1.32.13-eksbuild.16
vpc-cni compatible versions: v1.21.2-eksbuild.2, v1.22.1-eksbuild.2, v1.22.2-eksbuild.1, v1.22.3-eksbuild.1

eksUpgradeInsights.name: Cluster health issues
eksUpgradeInsights.description: Checks for any cluster health issues that prevent successful upgrade to the next Kubernetes version on EKS.
eksUpgradeInsights.recommendation: Call the EKS DescribeCluster API to view details and recommendations for remediating cluster health issues.
